4018.title: Cumulative confirmed COVID-19 cases
4018.subtitle: The number of confirmed cases is lower than the number of total cases. The main reason for this is limited testing.

4019.title: Daily confirmed COVID-19 cases
4019.subtitle: The number of confirmed cases is lower than the number of total cases. The main reason for this is limited testing.

4020.title: Total confirmed COVID-19 deaths
4020.subtitle: Limited testing and challenges in the attribution of the cause of death means that the number of confirmed deaths may not be an accurate count of the true number of deaths from COVID-19.

4021.title: Daily confirmed COVID-19 deaths
4021.subtitle: Limited testing and challenges in the attribution of the cause of death means that the number of confirmed deaths may not be an accurate count of the true number of deaths from COVID-19.

4028.subtitle: The number of confirmed cases is lower than the number of total cases. The main reason for this is limited testing.

4029.subtitle: Limited testing and challenges in the attribution of the cause of death means that the number of confirmed deaths may not be an accurate count of the true total number of deaths from COVID-19.

4030.title: Daily confirmed COVID-19 deaths per million people
4030.subtitle: Limited testing and challenges in the attribution of the cause of death means that the number of confirmed deaths may not be an accurate count of the true number of deaths from COVID-19.

display.name: Daily new confirmed cases per million

4056.note: Only countries with more than 100 confirmed cases are included.
4056.title: Case fatality rate of the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic
4056.subtitle:
The Case Fatality Rate (CFR) is the ratio between confirmed deaths and confirmed cases.
During an outbreak of a pandemic the CFR is a poor measure of the mortality risk of the disease. We explain this in detail at OurWorldInData.org/Coronavirus
4056.internalNotes: Only includes countries with >100 cases. Can be shared publicly.

relatedQuestions.text: What is the mortality risk of COVID-19?

4162.note: Comparisons of testing data across countries are affected by differences in the way the data are reported. Details can be found at our Testing Dataset page.
4162.title: Total COVID-19 tests per 1,000 people
4162.subtitle: The figures shown relate to the closest date for which we have data, with a maximum of 10 days' difference.
4162.sourceDesc: Official sources collated by Our World in Data

4191.note: Comparisons of testing data across countries are affected by differences in the way the data are reported. Daily data is interpolated for countries not reporting testing data on a daily basis. Details can be found at our Testing Dataset page.
4191.title: Daily COVID-19 tests per thousand people
4191.subtitle: The figures are given as a rolling 7-day average.

4193.title: Week by week change of confirmed COVID-19 deaths
4193.subtitle: The weekly growth rate on any given date measures the percentage change in number of confirmed deaths over the last seven days relative to the number in the previous seven days.

4194.title: Week by week change in confirmed COVID-19 cases
4194.subtitle: The weekly growth rate on any given date measures the percentage change in the number of new confirmed cases over the last seven days relative to the number in the previous seven days.

4195.title: Weekly confirmed COVID-19 cases
4195.subtitle: Weekly confirmed cases refer to the cumulative number of cases over the previous week.

4196.title: Weekly confirmed COVID-19 deaths
4196.subtitle: Weekly confirmed deaths refer to the cumulative number of confirmed deaths over the previous week.

4197.note: Comparisons of testing data across countries are affected by differences in the way the data are reported. Daily data is interpolated for countries not reporting testing data on a daily basis. Details can be found at our Testing Dataset page.
4197.title: Tests conducted per new confirmed case of COVID-19
4197.subtitle: Shown is the daily number of tests for each new confirmed case. This is a rolling 7-day average.

4198.note: Comparisons of testing data across countries are affected by differences in the way the data are reported. Daily data is interpolated for countries not reporting testing data on a daily basis. Details can be found at our Testing Dataset page
4198.title: The share of  COVID-19 tests that are positive
4198.subtitle: The daily positive rate, given as a rolling 7-day average.

4258.note: Only countries for which testing data is available are included. Details about this data can be found at OurWorldInData.org/coronavirus-testing.
4258.title: Daily new confirmed cases of COVID-19
4258.subtitle:
The line is blue when the rate of positive tests in a country is low – this means a country performs many tests relative to the size of the outbreak.
Red indicates a high positive rate of tests. This suggests that the true number of infections may be far higher than the number of confirmed cases.

142601.s_name: Official data collated by Our World in Data

source.name: Official data collated by Our World in Data
source.additionalInfo:
Data on COVID-19 testing. Comparisons between countries are compromised for several reasons.

You can download the full dataset, alongside detailed source descriptions here: https://github.com/owid/covid-19-data/tree/master/public/data/

142721.name: cumulative_positivity_rate
142721.description: The total number of confirmed cases to date divided by the total number of tests to date, expressed as a percentage. Tests may refer to the number of tests performed or the number of people tested – depending on which is reported by the particular country.
142721.s_name: Official data collated by Our World in Data

source.name: Official data collated by Our World in Data
source.additionalInfo:
Data on COVID-19 testing. Comparisons between countries are compromised for several reasons.

You can download the full dataset, alongside detailed source descriptions here: https://github.com/owid/covid-19-data/tree/master/public/data/

142754.name: short_term_tests_per_case
142754.description:
The number of tests each day divided by the number of confirmed cases each day.

The series is smoothed by averaging daily figures over a rolling 7-day window.

Not all countries report testing data on a daily basis. To generate this series we assume that testing changed equally on a daily basis over any periods in which no data was reported.
142754.s_name: Official data collated by Our World in Data

source.name: Official data collated by Our World in Data
source.additionalInfo:

145450.name: Total confirmed cases of COVID-19
145450.s_name: Johns Hopkins University CSSE COVID-19 Data – Last updated 21 December, 06:06 (London time)

source.name: Johns Hopkins University CSSE COVID-19 Data – Last updated 21 December, 06:06 (London time)
source.dataPublishedBy: COVID-19 Data Repository by the Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE) at Johns Hopkins University
source.additionalInfo: Raw data on confirmed cases and deaths for all countries is sourced from the COVID-19 Data Repository by the Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE) at Johns Hopkins University. Our complete COVID-19 dataset is a collection of the COVID-19 data maintained by Our World in Data. It is updated daily and includes data on confirmed cases, deaths, hospitalizations, and testing. We have created a new description of all our data sources. You find it at our GitHub repository here. There you can download all of our data.
